title: Hidden beneath Antarctic ice, scientists uncover a living river-like ecosystem
summary_full: Scientists have discovered a thriving estuarine ecosystem beneath Antarctica's Ross Ice Shelf, teeming with diverse microorganisms. This sub-ice environment, characterized by flowing waters and nutrient circulation, exists despite complete darkness, challenging previous perceptions of the continent's interior. The findings highlight life's adaptability in extreme conditions and its potential impact on ice dynamics and ocean circulation.
